title: PressMove Properties
description:
PressMove Properties

PressMove Properties

The following list is a summary of properties for the PressMove object.  The details for each property are explained in the Description field.

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to objects.  When PressMove object is created, name is assigned automatically.  For the assigned name, number is added after PressMove such as PressMove01.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for objects.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

Enabled Property	This property can set whether to enable the object.  When specifying True, the program executes the object.  When specifying False, the program executes the next object without executing the object.  Use this property when you want to save the sequence temporary without executing it or try with different parameters by copying objects during sequence creation.

AutoStepID Property	This property can set an automatic assignment of StepID in object execution.  When specifying True, assign StepID automatically.  When specifying False, it will be the value specified by StepID Property.  When assigning automatically, (sequence number *100 + object number) is set.  StepID is recorded to files that the force or the position during the sequence execution is recorded.  It is used to determine which interval corresponds to which object.

StepID Property	This property can set StepID during the object execution.  It is only used when AutoStepID is False.

AbortSeqOnFail Property	This property can set operations when the object fails.  When specifying True, the program ends the sequence and proceeds to the next SPEL statement if the object fails.  When specifying False, the program proceeds to the next force guide object without ending the sequence even if the object fails.  It is used when you want to continue the sequence (ex: the recovery processes are included in the sequence when the object fails.)

IOPreprocessEnabled Property	This property can set I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying True, the program executes I/O operation.  When specifying False, the program does not execute I/O operation.  I/O operations are defined by IOPreprocOutputBit Property and IOPreprocOutputStatus Property.  It is used when you want to operate the hands or peripherals before the object execution.

IOPreprocOutputBit Property	This property can set I/O operations (output bit) when the object starts.  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

IOPreprocOutputStatus Property	This property can set output status in I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying On, set the specified output bit to On (set to 1).  When specifying Off, set the specified output bit to Off (set to 0).  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

AccelS Property	This property can set the acceleration during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the acceleration for the set trajectory.  The actual acceleration is adjusted by the force control functions.

AccelR Property	This property can set the angular acceleration during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the angular acceleration for the set trajectory.  The actual angular acceleration is adjusted by the force control functions.

SpeedS Property	This property can set the speed during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the speed for the set trajectory.  The actual speed is adjusted by the force control functions.

SpeedR Property	This property can set the angular velocity during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the angular velocity for the set trajectory.  The actual angular velocity is adjusted by the force control functions.

RelativeOrient Property	This property can set the coordinate system that moves relatively.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

RelativeRobotLocal Property	This property can set the number of local coordinate system for the coordinate system that moves relatively.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType and Local in RelativeOrient.

DestRelativeX Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeY Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeZ Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeU Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in U direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeV Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in V direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeW Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in W direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeX Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeY Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeZ Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeU Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in U direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeV Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in V direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeW Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in W direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

CFEnabled Property	This property set whether to continue the force control functions after the objects are ended.  When specifying True, the force control functions will continue even if the objects are ended.  When specifying False, the force control functions will end when the objects are ended.  When the sequence ends even if CFEnabled is True, the force control functions will end.
